answer:
**In the Nāradasmṛti (also known as Nārada Smṛti), Chapter 1 ("Mātṛkā 1"), the text introduces legal procedure (vyavahāra) as having "eighteen topics" (vyavahārapadāni), which are the foundational "titles of law" (or heads of litigation).**[^1]

**These eighteen titles are enumerated explicitly across paragraphs Nar. M1.16–19 as follows:**

1. Non-payment of debt (ṛṇapratiṣvedhaḥ)  
2. Deposits (nikṣepaḥ)  
3. Partnership (samayasaṃyogaḥ or sainyam)  
4. Resumption of a gift (dattāpratigrahaṇaṃ or dattasyānumodana)  
5. Refusal to render service after agreeing to it (avijñāpanā or pratyayaḥ)  
6. Non-payment of wages (karmakaraḥ or vetana)  
7. Sale by one who is not the owner (asvāmivikrayāḥ)  
8. Non-delivery of a sold article (vikrītasya parihāraḥ)  
9. Rescission of a purchase (krayavikrayānuśayanaṃ)  
10. Breach of an agreement (sthulatyāgaḥ)  
11. Disputes concerning land (bhūmivivādaḥ)  
12. The relationship between man and woman (strīpumsaḥ)  
13. Inheritance (dāyabhāgaḥ)  
14. Crimes of violence (sāhasam)  
15. Verbal assault (vākpāruṣyam)  
16. Physical assault (daṇḍapāruṣyam)  
17. Gambling and betting (dyūtaḥ)  
18. Miscellaneous matters (prakīrṇakam)[^2][^3][^4][^5]

**The remainder of the chapter provides the detailed rules governing each title in sequence (e.g., detailed procedures for debt in Title 1, deposits in Title 2, etc.).** This list serves as the authoritative framework, with the text declaring a "hundred branches" (śākhāśata) as subdivisions arising from these eighteen.[^1]

[^1]: Nāradasmṛti, Chapter 1, [Nar. M1.9](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-79e060).
[^2]: Nāradasmṛti, Chapter 1, [Nar. M1.16–17](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-99f176).
[^3]: Nāradasmṛti, Chapter 1, [Nar. M1.18](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-a08b87).
[^4]: Nāradasmṛti, Chapter 1, [Nar. M1.19](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-d12f61).
[^5]: Nāradasmṛti, Chapter 1, [Mātṛkā 1 overview (¶¶ 1–20)](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-287f20).
